name: Product Management
description: The internal department responsible for owning and maintaining the FirstUK Bank Product Catalogue document, as explicitly stated in the metadata.

name: Director of Product Management
description: The named leadership role holding ownership and accountability for the Product Catalogue document within FirstUK Bank.

name: Customer Service
description: An internal operational team identified as a primary audience for the Product Catalogue, indicating its role in customer-facing operations and support.
context: Audience | Sales Staff, Customer Service, Risk, Compliance, Technology

name: Risk
description: A governance and control department within FirstUK Bank designated as a key audience for the Product Catalogue, responsible for risk oversight.
context: Audience | Sales Staff, Customer Service, Risk, Compliance, Technology

name: Compliance
description: A regulatory oversight department within FirstUK Bank listed as a primary audience for the Product Catalogue, ensuring adherence to internal and external standards.
context: Audience | Sales Staff, Customer Service, Risk, Compliance, Technology

name: Technology
description: The internal technology and engineering department identified as a key audience for the Product Catalogue, responsible for system implementation and support.
context: Audience | Sales Staff, Customer Service, Risk, Compliance, Technology

name: Sales Staff
description: Frontline employee group designated as a primary audience for the Product Catalogue, indicating their need for product knowledge to support client sales.
context: Audience | Sales Staff, Customer Service, Risk, Compliance, Technology

name: Financial Conduct Authority
description: The UK financial regulatory authority whose handbook (COBS) is referenced as a governing regulatory framework for FirstUK Bank's product documentation.
context: Regulatory References | FCA Handbook (COBS), PRA Rulebook

name: Prudential Regulation Authority
description: The UK prudential regulatory authority whose rulebook is cited as a key compliance framework governing FirstUK Bank's operations and product standards.
context: Regulatory References | FCA Handbook (COBS), PRA Rulebook

name: FirstUK Bank
description: The primary retail banking institution offering a focused suite of products designed for UK consumer customers. It currently manages a portfolio value of £3.4M across 500 customers and 800 active accounts.
context: FirstUK Bank offers a focused suite of retail banking products designed for UK consumer customers.

name: SmartSaver Account
description: A flexible, easy-access savings product serving as the primary offering for UK consumer customers. The product is characterized by attractive features and streamlined digital onboarding processes.
context: The primary product is the **SmartSaver Account**, a flexible, easy-access savings product with attractive features and streamlined digital onboarding.

name: Total Active Accounts
description: The total count of currently active accounts within the bank's retail portfolio. This metric is currently recorded at 800.

name: Total Customers
description: The total number of unique customer parties associated with the bank's retail portfolio. This metric is currently recorded at 500.

name: Average Account Balance
description: The mean monetary value held across all active accounts in the retail portfolio. This performance indicator is currently measured at £4,250.

name: Product Portfolio Value
description: The total monetary valuation of the bank's current retail product portfolio. This financial metric is currently recorded at £3.4M.

name: SmartSaver Account
description: A savings product launched in 2018 and currently marked as active within the FirstUK Bank portfolio. Functions as a core retail deposit offering with an established customer base and associated account structures.

name: SmartSaver Plus
description: A premium savings product introduced in 2021 and currently active. Functions as a higher-tier deposit offering designed to capture specific customer segments within the bank's portfolio.

name: Current Account
description: A transactional banking product launched in 2020 and currently active. Provides everyday payment and deposit functionality for customers through dedicated account arrangements.

name: Mortgage
description: A lending product introduced in 2022 and currently active. Delivers long-term credit financing to customers, structured through dedicated account frameworks within the bank's portfolio.

name: Debit Card
description: A payment product launched in 2018 and currently active. Serves as a transactional instrument enabling customer spending and cash access linked to underlying deposit accounts.

name: SmartSaver Account
description: A deposit savings product launched on 2026-01-15, currently marked as Active. It is formally classified under FCA COBS guidelines and restricted to general consumers aged 18 and over.

name: Deposit Product
description: The official regulatory classification assigned to the SmartSaver Account, dictating its operational and compliance framework under financial conduct authority standards.

name: FCA COBS Regulatory Framework
aliases: Financial Conduct Authority Consumer Protection Rules
description: The regulatory classification standard governing how the SmartSaver Account must be categorized and operated, mandating compliance with Financial Conduct Authority guidelines.

name: Age Eligibility Constraint
description: A strict eligibility rule limiting account access to general consumers who are at least 18 years of age.

name: Savings Account
description: A flexible, easy-access deposit product designed for straightforward savings. It is characterized by competitive interest rates, zero monthly fees, and a digital-first onboarding process, targeting customers seeking liquidity and ease of access to funds.
context: A flexible, easy-access savings account with competitive interest rates, no monthly fees, and digital-first onboarding.
Designed for customers who want straightforward savings with easy access to funds.

name: Interest Rates
description: A core banking concept representing the yield applied to customer deposits. In this product context, they are explicitly described as competitive and serve as a primary value proposition for the savings account.
context: A flexible, easy-access savings account with competitive interest rates, no monthly fees, and digital-first onboarding.

name: Customers
description: The target party segment for the savings account product. Defined in this document as individuals seeking straightforward savings solutions with easy access to their deposited funds.
context: Designed for customers who want straightforward savings with easy access to funds.

name: FirstUK Branch Network
description: Physical retail locations serving as customer-facing points for account onboarding procedures and service inquiries.
context: Branch Access: Account opening and queries at any FirstUK branch (see [[branches]])

name: Online Banking Platform
description: Web-based digital channel providing full account management capabilities and remote customer access across browser environments.
context: Online Banking: Full digital access via mobile and web

name: Mobile Banking Application
description: Smartphone application delivering full account functionality with integrated biometric authentication for secure user access.
context: Mobile Banking: Dedicated mobile app with biometric authentication

name: Joint Account
description: Shared account structure supporting multiple authorized holders, with ownership and operational rules defined in the bank's business ontology.
context: Joint Accounts: Support for joint account holders (see [[business_ontology]])

name: Standing Order
description: Automated payment instruction enabling customers to schedule and execute recurring fund transfers between accounts.
context: Standing Orders: Set up regular transfers to other accounts

name: Direct Debit
description: Pre-authorized collection mechanism allowing automatic fund extraction for recurring bills and subscription services.
context: Direct Debits: Automatic payment setup for bills and subscriptions

name: Debit Card
description: Account-linked payment instrument facilitating cash withdrawals at ATMs and point-of-sale online purchases.
context: Debit Card: Linked debit card for ATM withdrawals and online purchases

name: Variable Interest Rate
description: Non-fixed pricing mechanism applied to account balances, with accrued interest distributed to the customer on a monthly basis.
context: Competitive Interest: Variable interest rate, paid monthly

name: Account
description: A core banking product entity defined by specific operational and financial specifications. It operates in GBP currency with an indefinite tenure until closure, and supports variable interest accrual paid monthly.

name: Minimum Opening Balance
description: A hard operational constraint requiring a minimum initial deposit of £1 to establish the account. Prevents account creation below this threshold.

name: Maximum Balance
description: An unlimited upper limit on account holdings, allowing unrestricted deposit accumulation without balance caps or tiered restrictions.

name: Interest Rate
description: A variable rate applied to the account balance, governed by the external Interest Rate Schedule IRS-2026-001. Paid out on a monthly frequency.
context: Interest Rate | Variable (see Interest Rate Schedule IRS-2026-001)

name: Debit Card
description: A payment instrument permanently linked to the account, specifically issued as a Visa Debit card for customer transactions and withdrawals.

name: Account Opening Method
description: Defines the authorized channels for account creation, restricted to Online, Branch, or Postal methods. Excludes other onboarding pathways.
context: Opening Method | Online, Branch, or Postal

name: Interest Payment Frequency
description: A fixed operational rule mandating that accrued interest is calculated and disbursed to the account holder on a monthly basis.

name: Account Currency
description: The designated legal tender for the account, strictly set to British Pound Sterling (GBP) for all transactions and balance calculations.

name: Account Tenure
description: The operational lifespan of the account, defined as indefinite until explicitly closed by the bank or customer.

name: Interest Rate Structure
description: The overarching pricing framework defining how interest is calculated and applied to customer deposits, structured around a base rate and segmented balance thresholds.

name: Base Rate
description: The foundational interest rate applied to the lowest deposit tier, serving as the baseline for all subsequent tiered pricing calculations.

name: Annual Equivalent Rate
description: The standardized metric used to express the effective annual rate of return on deposits, accounting for compounding effects.

name: Rate Tiers
description: The segmented balance thresholds that determine which interest rate applies to a customer's deposit, ranging from £0 up to £50,001 and above.

name: Bank of England
description: The UK's central bank and monetary authority, whose base rate directly dictates the monthly review and adjustment of the bank's deposit interest rates.
context: linked to Bank of England base rate

name: Interest Rate Variation Policy
description: The governance rule mandating that the applied interest rate must be reviewed on a monthly basis and adjusted in direct correlation with external central bank rates.
context: **Variation Policy:** Rate reviewed monthly, linked to Bank of England base rate

name: Deposit Balance Threshold Tier 1
description: The lower deposit balance threshold bracket that qualifies for the base interest rate application.

name: Deposit Balance Threshold Tier 2
description: The mid-range deposit balance threshold bracket that qualifies for the intermediate interest rate application.

name: Deposit Balance Threshold Tier 3
description: The upper deposit balance threshold bracket that qualifies for the highest interest rate application.

name: Interest Rate Schedule
description: The authoritative reference document or system repository that formally publishes and maintains the bank's interest rate structure and tiered pricing rules.

name: SmartSaver
description: SmartSaver is a FirstUK Bank banking product designed to support joint account structures. It accommodates up to two registered account holders and operates under specific regulatory and operational frameworks defined in the business ontology.
context: SmartSaver supports joint accounts with up to 2 account holders.

name: Joint Account
description: A Joint Account is an account structure defined within the FirstUK Bank business ontology that allows multiple parties to share ownership. The structure enforces shared liability and specific withdrawal permissions for all registered holders.
context: SmartSaver supports joint accounts with up to 2 account holders.

name: Account Holder
description: An Account Holder, also referred to as a Signatory in this context, is an individual party registered on a joint account. This role carries shared ownership rights and is subject to joint liability for the account's balance and operations.
context: Either signatory can withdraw full balance

name: Joint and Several Liability
description: Joint and Several Liability is a regulatory constraint that applies to all holders of a joint account. It mandates that each holder is collectively and individually responsible for the account's obligations, ensuring full coverage of the balance regardless of individual contributions.
context: Regulatory treatment: Both holders jointly and severally liable

name: Full Balance Withdrawal Authority
description: Full Balance Withdrawal Authority is an operational rule governing transaction permissions on joint accounts. It grants any single signatory the unilateral authority to withdraw the entire account balance without requiring explicit consent from other registered holders.
context: Either signatory can withdraw full balance

name: Complaints Policy Dispute Resolution
description: Complaints Policy Dispute Resolution is a compliance requirement that dictates how customer disputes must be handled. All resolution processes must strictly adhere to the procedures outlined in the bank's formal Complaints Policy document, identified as COM-POL-006.

name: Total Balance Interest Calculation
description: Total Balance Interest Calculation is a product configuration rule that defines how interest accrues on joint accounts. The system calculates interest based on the aggregate balance of all account holders rather than splitting or attributing portions to individual parties.

name: Dual Signatory Onboarding Requirement
description: Dual Signatory Onboarding Requirement is an operational constraint governing the account creation process. It mandates that both designated signatories must be formally present and verified during the initial account opening procedure.

name: KYC Verification
description: A mandatory pre-onboarding process requiring identity, address, beneficial ownership, and source of funds checks to satisfy account opening requirements. The process is formally governed by a dedicated standard operating procedure.
context: - Identity verification (UK Passport or Driving License)
- Address verification (Utility bill, Council tax, or Bank statement)

name: AML Screening
description: A mandatory risk assessment process applied during account opening to screen customer names against sanctions lists, check for Politically Exposed Person status, and assess adverse media. The screening framework is governed by the bank's AML Policy.
context: - Customer name screening against sanctions lists

name: Customer
description: The individual or legal entity undergoing verification and screening processes during the account opening workflow, with their name subject to mandatory sanctions and PEP checks.
context: Customer name screening against sanctions lists

name: Account
description: The financial product being opened, subject to specific verification triggers such as beneficial ownership checks for business structures and source of funds validation for large deposits.

name: Identification Document
description: Official government-issued or financial records mandated to verify identity and residential address during the KYC process. Includes UK passports, driving licenses, utility bills, council tax notices, and bank statements.
context: Identity verification (UK Passport or Driving License)
Address verification (Utility bill, Council tax, or Bank statement)

name: Deposit Threshold >£10,000
description: A quantitative monetary threshold that triggers mandatory source of funds verification for any deposit exceeding ten thousand pounds during the account opening process.

name: KYC Verification SOP Requirement
description: A hard operational constraint mandating that all account applicants undergo identity, address, beneficial ownership, and source of funds verification prior to onboarding, as defined in KYC-SOP-001.
context: - Identity verification (UK Passport or Driving License)
- Address verification (Utility bill, Council tax, or Bank statement)

name: AML Screening Policy Requirement
description: A mandatory regulatory obligation requiring customer names, PEP status, and adverse media to be screened against relevant lists before account activation, per AML-POL-002.
context: - Customer name screening against sanctions lists

name: Account Opening Process
description: The end-to-end workflow for onboarding new customers into FirstUK Bank, executed across multiple delivery channels with defined processing timelines and mandatory documentation requirements.

name: Online Channel
description: A digital customer-facing delivery channel used for submitting account opening applications, subject to a 1–2 business day processing timeline.

name: Branch Channel
description: A physical customer-facing delivery channel for account opening, operating under the same 1–2 business day processing timeline as the online channel.

name: Postal Channel
description: A mail-based customer-facing delivery channel for account opening, with a longer processing timeline of 5–7 business days due to physical document handling.

name: Account Opening Processing Time SLA
description: The maximum allowable timeframe for completing the account opening workflow, defined as 1–2 business days for online and branch channels, and 5–7 business days for postal submissions.

name: Terms & Conditions
description: The mandatory legal agreement document required during the account opening process, identified by internal reference code SAV-001-TC.

name: Product Information Document
description: A mandatory informational document provided to customers during account opening, detailing product features and terms, tracked under reference code SAV-001-PID.

name: Account Opening SOP
description: The standardized operating procedure governing the execution, validation, and approval steps of the account opening workflow, serving as the primary control document for this process.

name: Online Banking
description: The primary digital self-service channel for FirstUK Bank customers, enabling account access, transaction monitoring, and payment configuration. It serves as the central interface for managing customer financial interactions without branch or call centre intervention.

name: Real-time Balance View
description: A digital banking capability that provides customers with immediate, up-to-date visibility of their account balances. This feature ensures financial awareness is maintained without processing delays or batch-settlement lag.

name: Transaction History
description: A record-keeping and reporting function within the online banking platform that retains and displays past account movements. It enforces a defined data retention window to support customer reconciliation and audit trails.

name: Standing Order Setup
description: A customer-facing configuration tool that enables the creation and scheduling of recurring fixed-amount payments to designated beneficiaries. It automates outbound fund transfers based on predefined dates and amounts.

name: Direct Debit Management
description: A control mechanism allowing customers to monitor, modify, or cancel authorized pull-based payment arrangements initiated by third-party payees. It provides oversight and revocation capabilities for recurring debit mandates.

name: Electronic Statements
description: A digital delivery method for account statements that replaces paper-based reporting. It provides customers with accessible, searchable financial documentation delivered electronically through the banking platform.

name: Mobile App
description: The primary customer-facing digital channel for FirstUK Bank, serving as the central interface for secure authentication, transaction monitoring, and account servicing. It enables customers to manage their banking interactions digitally through a dedicated mobile interface.

name: Biometric Login
description: A secure authentication mechanism integrated into the mobile banking channel, utilizing fingerprint and facial recognition technologies to verify customer identity during digital access.

name: Push Notifications
description: A real-time communication feature that delivers immediate transaction updates directly to the customer's mobile device, enhancing transparency and security awareness.

name: Mobile Cheque Deposit
description: A planned digital capability allowing customers to deposit physical cheques via the mobile application, currently designated as a future rollout item for the banking channel.

name: Card Management
description: A digital servicing function enabling customers to control their payment cards directly through the mobile interface, including capabilities for blocking and requesting replacements.

name: Account Alerts
description: A configurable notification system that triggers alerts when account balances cross predefined thresholds, providing customers with proactive financial oversight and balance monitoring.

name: Branch Services
description: Defines the operational scope and service catalog delivered through physical branch locations, encompassing customer-facing transactional and support functions.

name: Teller Access System
description: The system interface or channel enabling branch staff to process cash deposit and withdrawal transactions for customers.

name: Account Enquiries Service
description: A customer service function allowing account holders to request information regarding their account status, balances, and transaction history.

name: Card Replacement Service
description: An operational service for issuing new physical or virtual cards to replace lost, stolen, or damaged payment instruments.

name: Dispute Resolution Process
description: A dedicated service channel and workflow for investigating, validating, and resolving customer complaints or unauthorized transaction claims.

name: Complex Transaction Query Support
description: A specialized support function designed to handle non-standard, high-value, or multi-step payment and transfer requests that exceed routine processing parameters.

name: Monthly Maintenance Fee
description: A recurring monthly charge applied to the customer's account for standard account servicing and administration. The document explicitly defines a zero-cost structure, indicating that no fee is levied on customers for this service.

name: Overdraft Facility
description: A short-term credit facility allowing customers to withdraw funds beyond their available account balance. The document specifies that no overdraft facility is available for this product, with an explicit availability limit of £0.

name: ATM Withdrawals
description: Cash withdrawal transactions conducted at Automated Teller Machines linked to the customer's account. The document specifies unlimited usage rights with no associated transaction fees applied to these withdrawals.

name: International Transfers
description: Payment transactions initiated to transfer funds across national borders. The document specifies a fixed operational fee of £5 per transfer executed through this channel.

name: Card Replacement Service
description: A service allowing customers to obtain a new payment card in cases of loss, damage, or expiration. The document defines a tiered pricing structure where the first replacement is free, while subsequent replacements incur a £15 fee.
context: **Card Replacement:** £0 for first card, £15 for replacement

name: Debit Card
description: A payment instrument directly linked to the customer's account balance, enabling point-of-sale and ATM transactions. The document states that the card is provided free of charge as part of the standard product offering.

name: Pricing Guide
description: An internal reference system or documentation module containing detailed pricing structures, fee schedules, and product cost definitions for bank offerings. The text directs users to this guide for comprehensive pricing information.

name: Account
description: A core banking product entity whose operational status is explicitly defined by inactivity criteria. The policy establishes specific conditions under which an account transitions to dormant status, triggering automated procedural responses while preserving certain financial attributes for the customer.
context: An account is considered dormant if:
Full reactivation available upon customer request

name: Transaction
description: A financial movement entity used as a primary metric for determining account activity status. It specifically encompasses both deposits and withdrawals, serving as a key indicator of whether an account remains active or meets dormancy thresholds.
context: No transactions (deposits or withdrawals) for 12 months

name: Interest
description: A financial attribute that continues to accrue on an account even after it has been classified as dormant. This ensures customers retain earned funds during inactivity periods without requiring manual intervention or additional requests.

name: 12-Month Inactivity Threshold
description: A quantitative operational threshold defining the maximum allowable period of zero financial movements and zero customer engagement before an account is automatically classified as dormant. It establishes a fixed timeframe for both transactional activity and direct customer contact.
context: No transactions (deposits or withdrawals) for 12 months

name: Dormancy Classification Rule
description: A mandatory operational constraint that dictates the exact conditions under which an account transitions to dormant status. The rule requires simultaneous inactivity in transactions and customer contact over a fixed period, ensuring consistent status assignment across all accounts.
context: An account is considered dormant if:
No transactions (deposits or withdrawals) for 12 months

name: Dormancy Operational Procedures
description: A set of mandatory operational controls and customer-facing actions triggered upon dormancy classification. These procedures mandate external communication, system flagging, continued interest accrual, and provide a clear pathway for full account reactivation upon customer request.
context: Letter sent to last known address
Full reactivation available upon customer request

name: Online Banking
description: Digital channel through which customers can submit account closure requests. Serves as a primary submission point alongside physical and postal channels.
context: Submitted via online banking, branch, or mail

name: Branch Office
description: Physical location where customers may submit account closure requests in person. Acts as a secondary submission channel alongside digital and postal options.
context: Submitted via online banking, branch, or mail

name: Standing Order
description: Recurring payment instruction that must be explicitly cancelled as part of the account closure process to prevent unauthorized future transactions.

name: Direct Debit
description: Authorised payment arrangement that must be explicitly cancelled during account closure to ensure no further funds are drawn from the closing account.

name: Fraud Alert
description: Illicit activity that serves as a primary ground for bank-initiated account closure, alongside regulatory compliance issues and policy violations.
context: For regulatory reasons, fraud, or policy violation

name: Account Closure Policy
description: Governing rule defining the operational procedures, timelines, and conditions for both customer-initiated and bank-initiated account closures. Specifies balance handling methods and mandatory notice periods.

name: Account Closure Processing Time
description: Target timeframe for completing customer-initiated account closure requests, measured in business days.

name: Bank-Initiated Closure Notice Period
description: Mandatory advance notice period required before executing a bank-initiated account closure, ensuring customers are informed prior to finalisation.

name: Account Closure Standard Operating Procedure
description: Internal procedural system or repository containing the detailed workflow and compliance steps for executing account closures. Referenced as the authoritative source for closure execution.

name: Product Support
description: Internal organizational unit responsible for managing customer inquiries and providing assistance through multiple communication channels including phone, digital chat, email, and physical branches.

name: Customer Service Hours
description: Defined operational availability window for customer service delivery, specifying distinct operating hours for weekdays and Saturdays to ensure consistent support access.

name: Online Chat
description: Digital communication platform integrated with FirstUK Bank's support infrastructure, enabling real-time text-based customer assistance during designated business hours.
context: - Online Chat: Available during business hours

name: FirstUK branch
description: Physical retail locations operated by FirstUK Bank where customers can access in-person support and banking services as an alternative to digital or telephonic channels.

name: Active Accounts
description: Tracks the total number of currently active accounts associated with the product portfolio. Serves as a primary indicator of product adoption and ongoing customer engagement levels.

name: Total Customers
description: Measures the aggregate count of distinct customers linked to the product. Provides a baseline for evaluating market penetration and customer base growth.

name: Total Balance
description: Represents the combined monetary value held across all product accounts. Acts as a key financial performance indicator reflecting overall asset accumulation and revenue potential.

name: Average Account Balance
description: Calculates the mean balance per individual account within the product. Used to assess customer deposit behavior and identify opportunities for cross-selling or upselling.

name: Monthly New Accounts
description: Tracks the volume of newly opened accounts on a monthly basis. Serves as a leading indicator for product acquisition effectiveness and marketing campaign performance.

name: Account Closure Rate
description: Measures the percentage of accounts closed within a defined reporting period. Functions as a critical churn metric to evaluate product retention and customer satisfaction trends.

name: Customer Satisfaction (NPS)
description: Quantifies overall customer satisfaction using the Net Promoter Score framework. Provides actionable insights into service quality and identifies areas requiring operational improvement.

name: Regulatory Complaints
description: Tracks the frequency of formal complaints received from regulatory bodies on a quarterly basis. Monitors compliance health and operational risk exposure related to customer interactions.

name: Current Account
description: A core retail transactional account held by customers, identified in the document as a primary node for cross-selling linked products and services.

name: Debit Card
description: A payment instrument linked to customer accounts, listed as a frequently cross-sold product alongside current accounts and mortgages.

name: Mortgage
description: A long-term lending product used for property financing, identified as a key account that customers frequently link with current accounts and debit cards.

name: Insurance Products
description: A category of insurance offerings slated for future integration into the bank's cross-sell portfolio, positioned alongside existing account and card products.

name: SmartSaver Plus
description: A premium tier savings product designed for high-net-worth customers, identified by the internal product code SAV-002. Launched on 1 June 2021, it maintains an active status within the bank's retail banking portfolio.

name: High-net-worth Customer
description: A specific customer segment defined by significant asset holdings, qualifying for premium tier product access and specialized service levels within the bank's retail banking framework.

name: £50,000 Balance Threshold
description: A quantitative account balance requirement that serves as the primary qualification criterion for accessing premium tier products and associated customer benefits.

name: Base Interest Rate
description: The foundational interest rate applied to customer accounts, specified at 5.2% AER base as a key differentiator for the bank's offering.

name: Dedicated Relationship Manager
description: A dedicated staff member assigned to provide personalised support and oversight for customer accounts, serving as a key service differentiator.

name: Exclusive Financial Planning Platform
description: A dedicated digital application or suite of tools provided to customers to assist with financial planning and portfolio management.

name: Customer Headcount
description: The total number of active customers associated with the portfolio or segment, currently recorded at 120.

name: Account Volume Metric
description: The aggregate number of accounts held within the specified customer segment or portfolio, currently recorded at 150.

name: Portfolio Average Balance
description: The mean monetary value held across all accounts in the segment, calculated at £87,500.

name: Current Account
description: A retail banking product designed for general consumers and salary earners. Launched on 2020-03-15, it holds an active status within the bank's product portfolio and serves as a foundational transactional account type.

name: Online Banking System
description: Digital web-based channel enabling customers to access accounts, view balances, and execute transactions remotely.

name: Mobile Banking Application
description: Smartphone-based application providing customers with portable access to account management and transaction capabilities.

name: Standing Order
description: Pre-authorized payment instruction allowing customers to schedule recurring transfers from their accounts at fixed intervals.

name: Direct Debit
description: Payment method enabling third-party collectors to withdraw varying amounts from a customer's account with prior authorization.

name: Overdraft Facility
description: Optional credit arrangement allowing account holders to withdraw funds beyond their available balance, with a specified limit range of £500–£5,000.

name: Current Account Product
description: Standard transactional banking product offered with a zero monthly fee and comprehensive domestic transfer capabilities.

name: Cheque Book
description: Physical payment instrument provided to customers as an alternative or supplementary method for executing transactions.

name: Total Customer Headcount (320)
description: Quantitative measure of the active customer base utilizing the bank's services, currently recorded at 320.

name: Total Account Capacity (450)
description: Quantitative measure of all active banking accounts held by customers, currently recorded at 450.

name: Overdraft Facility
description: A core banking product that allows customers to withdraw funds beyond their available account balance. The facility is governed by strict eligibility criteria and features distinct pricing structures for pre-approved versus unauthorized usage.

name: Arranged Overdraft
description: A pre-authorized borrowing facility where the bank has agreed to a specific limit and interest rate. It is charged at a fixed annual equivalent rate and represents the standard tier of overdraft usage.

name: Unarranged Overdraft
description: An unauthorized borrowing facility incurred when account balances fall below zero without prior agreement. It is subject to a higher interest rate and incurs a daily administrative fee to reflect elevated credit risk.

name: Overdraft Eligibility Constraints
description: Mandatory operational rules governing customer qualification for the overdraft facility. Customers must satisfy a minimum account tenure of six months, demonstrate verified regular monthly income, and successfully pass a credit assessment before approval.

name: Overdraft Limit Range
description: The quantitative borrowing ceiling for the overdraft facility, defined as a customer-selectable range between £500 and £5,000. This metric establishes the maximum exposure limit per account based on customer preference.

name: Arranged Overdraft Interest Rate
description: The fixed annual equivalent rate applied to pre-approved overdraft balances. This metric standardizes the cost of borrowing for authorized usage at 15% per annum.

name: Unarranged Overdraft Interest Rate
description: The higher annual equivalent rate applied to unauthorized overdraft usage. This metric reflects elevated credit risk and is set at 25% per annum.

name: Daily Unarranged Fee
description: A fixed daily charge applied to unarranged overdraft balances as part of the pricing structure. This metric establishes a £5 per day administrative fee for unauthorized usage.

name: Mortgage Product
description: A core banking product offered by FirstUK Bank, identified internally as MORT-001. Launched on 2022-01-15 and currently operating in an Active status, it is specifically structured to serve property purchasers and existing homeowners.

name: Property Purchasers
description: A defined target customer segment for the Mortgage Product, representing individuals seeking to acquire property through FirstUK Bank's lending solutions.
context: **Target Audience:** Property purchasers and existing homeowners

name: Existing Homeowners
description: A defined target customer segment for the Mortgage Product, representing current property owners who may require refinancing or additional mortgage facilities.
context: **Target Audience:** Property purchasers and existing homeowners

name: Mortgage Product
description: A secured lending facility offered by FirstUK Bank, defined by specific borrowing limits, repayment durations, and interest rate structures contingent on borrower risk profiles.

name: Mortgage Loan Amount Range
description: The defined borrowing ceiling and floor for mortgage facilities, specifying a minimum of £50,000 and a maximum of £500,000 per facility.

name: Mortgage Repayment Term Range
description: The allowable duration for mortgage repayment, set between 2 and 25 years.

name: Mortgage Interest Rate Spectrum
description: The applicable interest rate range for the mortgage product, spanning 3.5% to 6.5%, with fixed terms of 2 or 5 years or variable structures.

name: Total Active Mortgages
description: The current portfolio count of active mortgage facilities held by the bank.

name: Total Mortgage Portfolio Balance
description: The aggregate outstanding principal value of the entire mortgage portfolio.

name: Average Mortgage Facility Size
description: The mean principal amount across all active mortgage facilities in the portfolio.

name: Underwriting Process
description: The core mortgage lending evaluation process that governs the assessment of borrower eligibility and risk prior to loan approval. This process requires specific documentation and analytical checks to ensure compliance with FirstUK Bank's lending criteria.

name: Proof of Income
description: A mandatory documentation requirement for mortgage underwriting, accepted in the form of either three years of accounts or three months of payslips to verify borrower earnings and repayment capacity.
context: - Proof of income (last 3 years accounts or 3 months payslips)

name: Property Valuation
description: A required assessment document used during the mortgage underwriting process to determine the market value and collateral security of the mortgaged property.

name: Proof of Deposit
description: A mandatory financial documentation requirement confirming the source and availability of the borrower's initial deposit funds for mortgage approval.

name: Credit Check
description: A mandatory risk assessment procedure performed during underwriting to evaluate the borrower's credit history, debt obligations, and overall repayment capacity.

name: Affordability Assessment
description: A required analytical evaluation during the underwriting process to determine whether the borrower's income and financial commitments can sustain the proposed mortgage repayments over the loan term.

name: Mortgage Underwriting Standard Operating Procedure
description: The governing standard operating procedure that defines the mandatory documentation requirements, procedural steps, and compliance controls for conducting mortgage underwriting at FirstUK Bank.

name: Mortgage Underwriting Documentation Requirement
description: A hard operational constraint mandating that all mortgage applications must be supported by specific proof documents, valuations, and assessments before underwriting approval can be granted.
context: - Proof of income (last 3 years accounts or 3 months payslips)

name: Debit Card
description: A core banking product representing a Visa-branded debit card issued by FirstUK Bank. The product identity specifications define it with Product ID CARD-001, a launch date of 2018-06-01, and an Active operational status.

name: Visa Debit Card
description: Defines the primary payment product specification for FirstUK Bank, identifying the underlying network scheme and product classification for customer issuance.

name: Contactless Transaction Limit
description: Establishes the maximum monetary value permitted for a single contactless payment transaction processed through the card network.

name: Daily ATM Withdrawal Limit
description: Sets the maximum aggregate amount that can be withdrawn from automated teller machines within a rolling 24-hour period.

name: Card Validity Period
description: Specifies the operational lifespan of the issued payment instrument before mandatory renewal or reissuance is required.

name: Card Replacement Fee Policy
description: Defines the financial cost associated with issuing a replacement card, including a specific exemption for the first replacement issued.

name: International Usage Authorization
description: Confirms the capability of the card to process transactions outside the domestic market via the Visa network infrastructure.

name: Card Material Specification
description: Details the physical composition of the payment instrument, specifying the use of recycled plastic for manufacturing.

name: PIN Length Requirement
description: Mandates the fixed character count for the personal identification number used to authenticate cardholder transactions.

name: Card
description: The primary payment instrument offered by FirstUK Bank, serving as the central subject for a suite of integrated security, transactional, and management capabilities.

name: Biometric Authentication
description: A security feature integrated into the mobile application that enables secure user verification and access control using biological identifiers.

name: Transaction Notifications
description: An alerting mechanism that delivers real-time updates regarding card activity to customers via push notifications and SMS channels.

name: Contactless Payment
description: A transaction capability allowing customers to complete purchases by tapping the card on compatible terminals without physical swiping or insertion.

name: ATM Withdrawals
description: A cash dispensing service accessible through automated teller machines across both UK and international networks.

name: Online Shopping
description: A payment use case supported by the card, enabling customers to conduct e-commerce transactions securely over the internet.

name: Virtual Card Number
description: A digitally generated payment credential designed specifically for securing online transactions and protecting the primary physical card details.

name: Card Lock/Unlock
description: A customer-controlled security function accessible via the mobile application that allows immediate suspension and restoration of card transaction capabilities.

name: Real-Time Fraud Detection
description: An automated monitoring capability that continuously analyzes card activity to identify and flag suspicious transactions as they occur.

name: Customer Liability Threshold
description: Defines the financial responsibility limit for customers in the event of fraud, specifying a £0 liability provided the incident is reported within a 24-hour window. This rule establishes the primary trigger for shifting financial risk from the customer to the bank and dictates the initial reporting requirement for claim eligibility.
context: - Customer liability: £0 if reported within 24 hours

name: Bank Liability Coverage
description: Establishes the bank's financial responsibility for fraudulent activity, mandating full (100%) coverage for all unauthorized transactions. This rule dictates the bank's risk exposure and reimbursement obligation when fraud occurs, ensuring complete customer protection against non-consensual charges.

name: Fraud Reimbursement Timeframe
description: Specifies the maximum operational deadline for processing and issuing fraud reimbursements following a customer claim submission. This constraint governs the speed of financial remediation in the fraud protection workflow and sets the service standard for claim resolution.
context: - Fraud reimbursement: Within 10 business days of claim

name: Total Cards Issued
description: Quantitative metric tracking the cumulative number of payment cards issued by FirstUK Bank to date. Serves as a baseline measure for portfolio size and issuance volume, providing context for active card utilization rates.

name: Active Cards
description: Operational metric indicating the number of issued cards currently in active use or status. Used alongside total issuance to monitor card portfolio health, track churn, and assess the effective reach of the bank's payment network.

name: Monthly Fraud Claims
description: Volume metric measuring the average number of fraud claims processed by the bank each month. Acts as a key performance indicator for fraud volume and operational workload, directly influencing resource allocation for claims handling and reimbursement processing.

name: Card
description: A core payment instrument issued by FirstUK Bank, tracked through issuance and active status metrics to monitor portfolio health. Serves as the primary vehicle for customer transactions subject to fraud protection rules, liability thresholds, and reimbursement workflows.

name: Unauthorized Transaction
description: A fraudulent or non-consensual financial transaction that triggers bank liability coverage and initiates the fraud protection and reimbursement workflow. Represents the core risk event addressed by the liability rules and serves as the basis for customer claims.

name: Fraud Claim
description: A formal customer report of fraudulent activity that serves as the trigger for liability assessment, investigation, and subsequent reimbursement processing. Acts as the operational input for the 10-business-day reimbursement rule and feeds into monthly fraud claim tracking.
context: - Fraud reimbursement: Within 10 business days of claim

name: Investment Account

context: | Investment Account | Design | Q3 2026 | Stocks, ETFs, ISA |

context: | Savings Bonds | Research | Q1 2027 | Fixed-term savings |

description: A business transactional account currently in the scoping phase. Intended to support SME operational banking needs and daily cash flow management. Planned for launch in Q2 2027.

name: Investment Account Target Launch Date
description: The scheduled operational launch timeline for the Investment Account product. Established as a key development milestone to ensure timely market entry and regulatory readiness.
context: | Investment Account | Design | Q3 2026 | Stocks, ETFs, ISA |

name: Travel Money Card Target Launch Date
description: The scheduled operational launch timeline for the Travel Money Card product. Defined as a development milestone to align multi-currency infrastructure deployment with market demand.
context: | Travel Money Card | Development | Q4 2026 | Multi-currency prepaid |

name: Savings Bonds Target Launch Date
description: The scheduled operational launch timeline for the Savings Bonds product. Set as a research-to-launch milestone to validate fixed-term savings structures before commercial rollout.

name: Small Business Account Target Launch Date
description: The scheduled operational launch timeline for the Small Business Account product. Established as a scoping completion target to ensure SME transactional capabilities are ready for deployment.
context: | Small Business Account | Scoping | Q2 2027 | Business transactional |

name: Product Committee
description: An internal governance committee responsible for conducting bi-weekly reviews of product-related matters within FirstUK Bank's operational framework. This body operates under the broader organisational model and ensures consistent oversight across all product lifecycle stages.

name: Board Risk Committee
description: A senior governance board that holds the formal decision authority for approving new products. It specifically oversees high-value offerings, requiring explicit sign-off when potential values exceed £1 million.

name: Product Approval Threshold
description: A governance constraint that mandates escalation to the Board Risk Committee for any product with a potential value exceeding £1 million. This rule ensures strict oversight and risk mitigation for high-value product launches.

name: Risk Appetite
description: The bank's defined strategic tolerance for risk exposure, explicitly established as conservative within the product governance framework. This stance directly influences capital planning and aligns with regulatory expectations for Pillar 1 requirements.

name: Pillar 1 Capital Adequacy Ratio
description: A quantitative regulatory and internal capital requirement target set at a minimum of 15%+. This threshold serves as the primary metric to enforce the bank's conservative risk appetite and maintain adequate capital buffers.

name: Financial Conduct Authority
description: The UK regulatory authority responsible for overseeing conduct of business standards, market integrity, and consumer protection within the financial services sector.

name: Prudential Regulation Authority
description: The UK regulatory body responsible for the prudential regulation and supervision of banks, building societies, and credit unions.

name: FCA Conduct of Business Sourcebook
description: The regulatory framework issued by the FCA that establishes mandatory conduct standards and operational rules governing the bank's business activities and client interactions.

name: PRA Prudential Rulebook
description: The regulatory framework issued by the PRA setting out prudential requirements, capital standards, and risk management obligations for the bank's operations.

name: General Data Protection Regulation
description: The data protection and privacy regulation establishing mandatory compliance requirements for the bank's processing, storage, and handling of personal data.

name: Open Banking Regulatory Framework
description: The regulatory framework governing open banking standards, third-party data sharing, and payment service directives applicable to the bank's digital services.
context: Open Banking regulations (PSD2, Open Banking Standard)

name: Product Information Document System
description: A centralized documentation framework used to maintain consistent product information across the bank's offerings.
context: Each product has a standardized Product Information Document (PID):

name: SmartSaver Account
description: A retail savings account product managed within the bank's portfolio, governed by a standardized PID.

name: SmartSaver Plus
description: An enhanced savings account variant within the bank's product suite, linked to its corresponding PID.

name: Current Account
description: A standard transactional account product for everyday banking, governed by a dedicated PID.

name: Mortgage
description: A property financing lending product, with its specifications documented in a standardized PID.

name: Debit Card
description: A payment instrument linked to customer accounts, requiring standardized product information documentation.

name: Product FAQ Repository
description: A centralized reference system providing frequently asked questions and product details for bank offerings.

name: Mandatory PID Documentation Rule
description: A mandatory operational rule requiring that every bank product must be accompanied by a standardized Product Information Document.
context: Each product has a standardized Product Information Document (PID):

name: SmartSaver Account
description: A savings account product governed by FirstUK Bank's specific Terms & Conditions (SAV-001-TC). The product documentation is subject to mandatory periodic review to ensure ongoing compliance and accuracy.

name: Current Account
description: A standard transactional account product managed by FirstUK Bank, with dedicated Terms & Conditions (CURR-001-TC) that require structured governance and periodic validation.

name: Mortgage
description: A lending product offered by FirstUK Bank, governed by specific Terms & Conditions (MORT-001-TC) that must be reviewed annually or immediately upon any structural product changes.

name: Product Terms Review Policy
description: A mandatory governance rule requiring all product-specific Terms & Conditions to be reviewed on an annual basis, with immediate re-evaluation triggered whenever a product undergoes changes.
context: Review Frequency: Annual (or when product changes)

name: Terms & Conditions Last Review Date
description: Tracks the most recent compliance review date for product documentation, serving as an audit metric to verify adherence to the annual review constraint and maintain regulatory readiness.

name: Product Head
description: The designated owner responsible for managing the product version history, overseeing updates to account details, rates, and new product tiers. Holds accountability for the lifecycle and commercial governance of the product catalogue.

name: Joint Account
description: A shared account structure added to the product offering, requiring specific details and likely dual ownership or authorization parameters within the bank's retail banking framework.
context: Added joint account details, updated rates

name: SmartSaver Plus
description: A specific savings tier introduced into the product catalogue, representing a distinct offering within the bank's retail portfolio that requires version tracking and rate management.

name: Mortgage
description: A secured lending product added to the catalogue, indicating an expansion of the bank's credit offerings and customer financing capabilities.

name: Investment Roadmap
description: A structured planning or offering related to investment products, added alongside mortgage capabilities to expand the customer's financial journey and product suite.

name: Interest Rate
description: The pricing mechanism for deposits or loans, subject to periodic updates and annual reviews as part of the product's commercial governance and version control process.

name: Product Catalogue
description: The central repository or listing of all available banking products and services, initially established in version 1.0 and continuously updated to reflect new offerings and rate changes.

name: Product Management Department
description: A core internal department responsible for product lifecycle management and strategy, referenced as a key governance and operational unit within the bank's organizational structure.

name: Departments Reference Catalog
description: A structural reference cataloging the bank's internal organizational units and governance bodies, used to map reporting lines, functional responsibilities, and cross-departmental dependencies.

name: Business Ontology
description: A foundational conceptual framework defining core product concepts, account types, and domain entities that standardize terminology, data structures, and business definitions across FirstUK Bank.

name: Interest Rate Schedule
description: A structured reference framework defining applicable interest rates, pricing tiers, and yield calculations for banking products, serving as a quantitative benchmark for commercial decisions and account configuration.

name: Account Opening Standard Operating Procedure
description: A mandatory procedural constraint governing the end-to-end workflow for creating new customer accounts, ensuring standardized onboarding steps, data validation, and compliance checks before activation.

name: KYC Verification Standard Operating Procedure
description: A formal governance procedure establishing mandatory controls and steps for Know Your Customer verification, ensuring regulatory compliance and identity validation prior to account activation.

name: Anti-Money Laundering Policy
description: A comprehensive policy framework defining mandatory controls, screening procedures, and reporting mechanisms to prevent, detect, and mitigate money laundering activities in accordance with regulatory standards.

name: Pricing Guide
description: A reference framework establishing approved pricing structures, fee schedules, and rate parameters for bank products, serving as a constraint for commercial decisions and product configuration.

name: Complaint Handling Policy
description: A governance framework outlining mandatory processes for logging, investigating, and resolving customer complaints to ensure service quality, accountability, and regulatory adherence.

name: Fraud Prevention and Detection Policy
description: A formal policy establishing operational controls, detection thresholds, and response protocols for identifying and mitigating fraudulent activities across bank operations and customer channels.

name: Director of Product Management
description: Senior leadership role responsible for product management governance and final approval authority within FirstUK Bank, as evidenced by the document sign-off requirement.

name: Chief Risk Officer
description: C-level executive role accountable for enterprise risk management and oversight at FirstUK Bank, designated as a required approver in the sign-off process.

name: Compliance Head
description: Department leadership role responsible for regulatory compliance and control functions within FirstUK Bank, required to authorize the document upon sign-off.
